sections.paragraph:
- Original: μώλωψ

- Transliteration: Molops

- Phonetic: mo'-lopes

- Definition:

1. a bruise, wale, wound that trickles with blood

- Origin: "from molos ("moil", probably akin to the base of G3433) and probably ops (the face, from G3700)"

- TDNT entry: 17:49,6

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: From μῶλος mō los (moil; probably akin to the base of G3433) and probably ὤψō ps (the face; from G3700); a mole (black eye) or blow mark: - stripe.
